summary: The mockup is now editable DOM with real assets, but the hero and learning-lab panels are taller than the ideal, so the proof band is pushed too low and is clipped in the first viewport.

majorDiffAreas.title: Hero panels push proof band below the intended fold
majorDiffAreas.expected: The resource rail and compact proof band appear directly below the hero within the desktop first viewport.
majorDiffAreas.actual: The editable hero, book cover, and lab panels are taller than the source composition; the proof claim begins near the bottom of the screenshot and is clipped.
majorDiffAreas.impact: The homepage loses the generated reference's immediate trust cue and the top fold feels more vertically stretched than the intended compact workbench.

evidence.observation: The proof band starts near y=765 and the '100+ universities and colleges' line is cut off at the bottom of the 900px capture.
Compared with the ideal, the right-side lab panel is much taller and the proof band is lower.

majorDiffAreas.recommendedChange: Compress the lab panel heights, book cover size, hero padding, and proof-band spacing so the proof claim and logos sit inside the first viewport.

matches: The mockup now uses editable DOM instead of rendering the generated source image as the page.
The nav, title, CTAs, book cover, lab panels, resource rail, and proof assets are visible and mapped to real local assets.
The console capture reports no browser errors.
Palette and content hierarchy are close enough for a compact polish pass.
Verified local logos and portraits replace generated social-proof marks.
uncertainties: Exact ideal micro-chart shapes cannot be matched perfectly because the editable mockup uses existing GIF assets rather than the generated chart stills.
possibleIntentionalDeviations: Using verified summary language instead of generated quote text is intentional.

inputQuality: Artifacts are sufficient for the next scoped CSS height/spacing polish.

nextIteration.focus: Compress the first viewport so the proof band appears immediately below the hero like the ideal.
steps: Reduce lab main panel heights, mini-panel image heights, and the book cover width.
Tighten hero padding and proof-band padding.
Rerun Limner compare and inspect the side-by-side.
avoidForNow: Do not replace verified assets with generated imagery.
Do not attempt pixel-perfect chart recreation in this pass.
nextIteration.expectedImprovement: The next screenshot should keep the editable page structure while moving the proof claim and logo strip higher into the first viewport.

rationale: The major scaffold problem is fixed; the next highest-value fidelity issue is vertical density. A CSS-only compression pass should improve alignment without changing content or asset provenance.
